exemplar.title: To what extent can Voronoi Diagrams be used to find the optimal location for a new library in North-West Kent?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can Voronoi Diagrams be used to find the optimal location for a new library in North-West Kent?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Research question is stated prominently and sets a clear focus.
Sections and sub-headings provide a recognisable structure.
Most content is directly relevant to the investigation of Voronoi diagrams.
weaknesses: Transitions are sometimes abrupt and justification for excluding vertices is weak.
Repetition of procedural steps and informal phrasing reduces conciseness.
Diagrams are low resolution and bibliography is embedded in the body, disrupting flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows some coherence and logical development but lacks polish in transitions and formal tone (Band 2). Organization is adequate with clear sections but gaps remain (Band 2). Content is mostly relevant yet contains unnecessary repetition (Band 2), justifying a middle score of 2 out of 4.

strengths: Basic mathematical vocabulary and notation are used correctly (e.g., vertex, gradient).
Maps, diagrams, and tables support the exploration.
Explanations are generally understandable to the reader.
weaknesses: Frequent typographical slips and inconsistent formatting diminish professionalism.
Axes and scales are unlabeled on figures, reducing interpretability.
Some formatting errors (HTML tags) and mismatches between text and figures disrupt consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication uses some appropriate mathematical language and notation but with inconsistent styling (Band 2). Representations are appropriate yet lack sophistication and clarity (Band 2), and overall clarity is mostly consistent but marred by formatting issues (Band 2), warranting 2 out of 4.

strengths: The choice of a local library siting problem shows some personal relevance.
Use of Google Maps to source data indicates a personal approach.
weaknesses: Exploration closely follows a standard textbook method with little adaptation.
No original mathematical ideas or deeper questioning are evident.
Personal initiative and creativity remain limited beyond routine procedures.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of independent thinking is limited and creativity is minimal (Band 1). The investigation shows some personal framing but lacks a clear personal drive (Band 1), and initiative is only at a basic level (Band 1), supporting a score of 1 out of 3.

strengths: The student recognises the limitation of a distance-only model.
Some links are made between results and real-world factors like population distribution.
weaknesses: Reflections are brief and mostly descriptive without deep critical analysis.
No quantitative or mathematical suggestions are proposed to address limitations.
Connections to understanding remain general and not integrated into the mathematics.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but limited in depth (Band 1). Critical analysis is basic with minimal critique (Band 1), and connections to enhanced understanding are only cursory (Band 1), justifying 1 out of 3.

strengths: Appropriate use of Voronoi diagrams, perpendicular bisectors, and distance calculations at course level.
Calculations of gradients, midpoints, and distances are correct and clearly shown.
Conceptual approach to finding the largest empty circle demonstrates understanding of the geometry involved.
weaknesses: Reliance on a calculator for solving intersections without at least one algebraic derivation.
No discussion of potential error margins introduced by rounding.
Mathematical rigour could be deepened with further justification of conventions (e.g., sign of gradient).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant at course level and used correctly (Band 3). There are no major errors and the work shows good understanding in parts (Band 3). However, it lacks the sophistication and rigour required for higher bands, so a mid-range score of 3 out of 6 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student follows a standard textbook approach to constructing a Voronoi diagram; little independent adaptation or deeper questioning is evident beyond applying the routine method.

The student shows clear understanding of how Voronoi cells relate to nearest-neighbour regions and how vertex-to-site distance yields the largest empty circle.  Explanations are sound but not deep or rigorous, placing the work in âgood understanding in parts.â
Gradients, mid-points and distances are computed correctly apart from minor rounding.  No systemic errors are observed, corresponding to âmathematics is mostly correct.â

Most material relates to locating a new library, yet there is repetition of procedural steps (the perpendicular-bisector process is described twice) and some digressions on national library usage.  The work is therefore âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

Basic mathematical vocabulary (vertex, perpendicular bisector, gradient) and standard notation are used correctly, but there are frequent typographical slips and inconsistent use of bold/italics.  Overall this is âsome appropriate mathematical language and notation.â

Explanations are generally understandable; however, formatting errors (e.g., stray HTML tags) and occasional mismatches between text and figure labels reduce consistency.  Thus communication is âmostly clear with some consistency.â

Voronoi diagrams, perpendicular bisectors, distance calculations and simple optimisation are all squarely within the AI SL syllabus and used appropriately to address the question.  The work is correct for the level but not extended, so it matches ârelevant mathematics at course level used.â

Maps, labelled diagrams of the Voronoi cells, and tables of vertex distances are supplied, but axes are not fully scaled and some images are blurred.  The representations help the reader but are not employed with sophistication, placing the work in the âsome appropriate representationsâ band.
The evaluation notes cost, population distribution, and land-use constraints, but the reflections are brief and descriptive rather than probing.  This corresponds to âlimited reflection present.â

Using Google Maps and overlaying a Voronoi is mildly innovative, yet no original mathematical technique is devised and the analysis stops once the largest empty circle is found.  Therefore only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is demonstrated.

The report is readable and the argument generally progresses from introduction, through construction of the Voronoi diagram, to a brief evaluation.  However, transitions are sometimes abrupt (e.g., the sudden move from graphical construction to Pythagoras without sign-posting) and justification for excluding certain vertices is weak, so the logical thread is only partially convincing.  This aligns best with âsome coherence and logical development.â

The student recognises that distance-only optimisation is flawed, yet does not analyse the mathematical implications or suggest quantitative improvements.  Such surface level comment fits âbasic analysis with limited critique.â

Comments about library usage illustrate some link between results and real-world understanding, but they remain general and are not integrated back into the mathematics; hence only a âlimited connection to understanding.â

Sections and sub-headings (Introduction, Mathematical Exploration, Evaluation) give the work a recognisable framework, but there are gaps (no explicit conclusion, bibliography embedded in the body).  Hence organisation is adequate rather than polished, matching the âadequate organisation with basic structureâ descriptor.

Choice of topic is personally framed (âimportant infrastructure in my areaâ) but the exploration largely mirrors generic examples.  Evidence of a personal stamp on decision-making or data selection is limited, fitting the âsome evidence of personal approachâ band.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated prominently and clearly, setting a focused aim for the investigation.
The opening map image lacks a caption or description of axes and scale, reducing its standalone clarity.
The introduction contains repetitive and informal language. Consider tightening sentences to improve conciseness and maintain academic tone.
Phrasing like âgood areas of learningâ is vague. Specify what metrics (e.g., user numbers or resources) define âgoodâ to strengthen your rationale.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent (e.g., HTML tags showing). Ensure all citations follow a consistent style to enhance readability.
Definitions of sites, cells, and vertices are clearly given but would benefit from explicit linkage to the coordinate methods used later.
The Voronoi diagram figure lacks axis labels and a scale bar, making it hard to interpret distances directly from the image.
The transition from defining y=mx+c to its application could be clearer if the general formula for computing perpendicular bisectors were stated first.
Gradient calculation is executed correctly, but note that rounding to 3 sf introduces an approximation; consider discussing potential error margins.
The negative reciprocal step is correct, but a brief justification of the sign convention would improve mathematical rigor.
The use of the midpoint formula is accurate and clearly presented, showing good application of coordinate geometry.
Combining multiple algebraic steps into one block (solving for c) makes it harder to follow; separate each operation for clarity.
The final perpendicular bisector equation $$y=-1.90x+19.4$$ is correctly derived and clearly stated.
The Voronoi diagram image is low resolution without a clear legend or labels for bisectors, reducing its communicative effectiveness.
The process for finding the largest empty circle is succinctly explained, demonstrating clear understanding of Voronoi vertex analysis.
Excluding vertices âwith four sitesâ is justified qualitatively but lacks quantitative criteria; define thresholds for exclusion.
Vertices are highlighted in the diagram but not numbered or linked to text; include labels or a reference table for clarity.
Listing vertex coordinates shows good use of analytical geometry, though a table format would improve readability.
Using the calculator (GDC) for solving intersections is efficient, but include at least one algebraic derivation to show understanding.
The conceptual approach of equating bisector equations demonstrates sound understanding of vertex intersection.
Substitution of x value into both bisector equations to confirm y shows good consistency checking.
The explanation of Pythagoras' theorem is correct but would be strengthened by a diagram illustrating the a, b, and c components.
Casual phrasing (âAll that needs to be doneâ) interrupts formal tone; revise to maintain academic style.
Distance calculation correctly applies $$c=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}$$ to get 3.93; this demonstrates solid use of Pythagorean theorem.
The axes and distance units are never labeled on diagrams, which could lead to misinterpretation of scale.
The identification of the optimal location (J) around Vâ is clearly justified by the largest distance value.
Linking the chosen location to nearby schools is pertinent, but demographic claims need supporting data and quantitative analysis.
The evaluation acknowledges distance-only limitations but does not propose mathematical adaptations (e.g., weighted Voronoi) to address geography.
The conclusion restates the finding but would benefit from a concise summary of key mathematical steps and reflections.
Bibliography entries are relevant but are embedded in the body; move this section to the end and format consistently.
